title: Ferolia brasiliensis

It belongs to the tribe Mutisieae, a group of plants that are primarily found in the Americas. intro: Ferolia brasiliensis, also known as Ferula brasiliensis, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Ferolia brasiliensis include ferulic acid, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
